prompt.content:
You are a spatial reasoning assistant analyzing a 3D scene image.
You will receive a list of objects visible in the image and a set of distance comparison questions.

For each question, compare 3D distances — either between two pairs of objects,
or from a common anchor object to two candidate objects.
Answer with exactly one of: "<" (first pair/candidate closer), "~=" (approximately equal), ">" (first pair/candidate farther).

Respond ONLY with a JSON array. Each element must have "qid" and "answer" (a string: "<", "~=", or ">").

Example:
[{"qid": "qrr_0001", "answer": "<"}, {"qid": "qrr_0002", "answer": "~="}]

content.text:
Objects in the image:
  - obj_0: large brown metal sphere
  - obj_1: small green metal cylinder
  - obj_2: large green rubber cylinder
  - obj_3: small blue metal cylinder
  - obj_4: large green metal cube
  - obj_5: small purple metal cube
  - obj_6: large green metal sphere
  - obj_7: large blue metal sphere
  - obj_8: large red metal cylinder
  - obj_9: large yellow metal cylinder

Questions:
[qrr_0141] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_6 vs the distance between obj_1 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0142] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_6 vs the distance between obj_2 and obj_3. Answer: < / ~= / >

[qrr_0150] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_6 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_8. Answer: < / ~= / >
[qrr_0151] Compare the distance between obj_0 and obj_6 vs the distance between obj_3 and obj_9. Answer: < / ~= / >

raw_response:
I need to analyze the 3D positions of objects in the scene to compare distances.

Let me identify the objects and their approximate positions:
- obj_0 (large brown metal sphere): right side, back area
- obj_1 (small green metal cylinder): center-right area
- obj_2 (large green rubber cylinder): center-back area
- obj_3 (small blue metal cylinder): left-front area
- obj_4 (large green metal cube): back-left area
- obj_5 (small purple metal cube): center-front area
- obj_6 (large green metal sphere): back-right area
- obj_7 (large blue metal sphere): front-center area
- obj_8 (large red metal cylinder): left-center area
- obj_9 (large yellow metal cylinder): right-front area

First, let me estimate the distance between obj_0 (brown sphere) and obj_6 (green sphere). They appear to be separated by a moderate distance, both in the back area but obj_0 is more to the right/front.

Looking at the image carefully:
- obj_0 (brown sphere) is on the right side, middle depth
- obj_6 (green sphere) is behind, left-center

The distance between obj_0 and obj_6 appears to be moderate (~3-4 units).

Now let me compare each pair:

[qrr_0141] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_1-obj_9: obj_1 (green small cylinder) and obj_9 (yellow cylinder) are close together on the right side. Distance obj_1-obj_9 seems small. obj_0-obj_6 > obj_1-obj_9. Answer: >

[qrr_0142] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_2-obj_3: obj_2 (green rubber cylinder) is center-back, obj_3 (blue cylinder) is left-front. Large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_2-obj_3. Answer: <

[qrr_0143] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_2-obj_4: obj_2 and obj_4 are both in the back area, relatively close. obj_0-obj_6 > obj_2-obj_4. Answer: >

[qrr_0144] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_2-obj_5: obj_2 is back-center, obj_5 is front-center. Large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_2-obj_5. Answer: <

[qrr_0145] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_2-obj_7: obj_2 is back, obj_7 is front. Large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_2-obj_7. Answer: <

[qrr_0146] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_2-obj_8: obj_2 is center-back, obj_8 is left-center. Moderate distance. ~= or <. Answer: ~=

[qrr_0147] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_2-obj_9: obj_2 is back-center, obj_9 is right-front. Large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_2-obj_9. Answer: <

[qrr_0148] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_3-obj_4: obj_3 is left-front, obj_4 is back-left. Large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_3-obj_4. Answer: <

[qrr_0149] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_3-obj_7: obj_3 is left-front, obj_7 is center-front. Small distance. obj_0-obj_6 > obj_3-obj_7. Answer: >

[qrr_0150] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_3-obj_8: obj_3 and obj_8 are both on the left. Moderate distance. ~= Answer: ~=

[qrr_0151] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_3-obj_9: obj_3 is left-front, obj_9 is right-front. Moderate-large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_3-obj_9. Answer: <

[qrr_0152] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_4-obj_5: obj_4 is back-left, obj_5 is front-center. Large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_4-obj_5. Answer: <

[qrr_0153] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_4-obj_7: obj_4 is back-left, obj_7 is front-center. Very large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_4-obj_7. Answer: <

[qrr_0154] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_4-obj_8: obj_4 is back-left, obj_8 is left-center. Moderate distance. obj_0-obj_6 ~= obj_4-obj_8. Answer: ~=

[qrr_0155] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_4-obj_9: obj_4 is back-left, obj_9 is right-front. Very large distance. obj_0-obj_6 < obj_4-obj_9. Answer: <

[qrr_0156] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_5-obj_7: obj_5 and obj_7 are both front-center, close. obj_0-obj_6 > obj_5-obj_7. Answer: >

[qrr_0157] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_5-obj_8: obj_5 is front-center, obj_8 is left-center. Moderate distance. ~= Answer: ~=

[qrr_0158] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_5-obj_9: obj_5 and obj_9 are close in front area. obj_0-obj_6 > obj_5-obj_9. Answer: >

[qrr_0159] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_7-obj_8: obj_7 is front-center, obj_8 is left-center. Moderate distance. ~= Answer: ~=

[qrr_0160] obj_0-obj_6 vs obj_7-obj_9: obj_7 is front-center, obj_9 is right-front. Small-moderate distance. obj_0-obj_6 > obj_7-obj_9. Answer: >
